title: What did lilith look and act like without the restraints given by nerv
display_title: What did lilith look and act like without the restraints given by nerv
wikitext:
[]: What did lilith look and act like without the restraints given by nerv??             Quote Post by Bob74h » Sun Jun 01, 2025 10:08 am We only ever see her restrained We see she is still alive As she instantly regenerates her legs once the spear is removed

[]: Re: What did lilith look and act like without the restraints given by nerv??             Quote Post by consentwastaken » Fri Jun 06, 2025 7:53 pm I'd put giant air quotes when you say "alive." Pre-Giant Naked Rei, Lilith doesn't have her soul, so it's a weird autonomous regeneration rather than being actually alive and consciously doing that. It's like how animals shake a whole bunch when they die. Sure, they're moving, but that doesn't mean they're alive (I would bring up Mike the Headless Chicken, but its intact brainstem still means he's alive). But that's besides the point. There's almost no telling what Lilith looked like before. It's interesting to note that the state they found Adam in Antarctica is very similar looking to the Lilith we find in Central Dogma. We can't see the face, but as far as its body goes, it's the same; they're both large, pale, and somewhat chubby. So they're restrained versions look similar, but considering Adam went from that to this: Who the hell knows what Lilith looked like. The only educated guess you can make is that it's somewhat Similar to Giant Naked Rei in EoE, but what would that even mean? Lilith wouldn't have the facial features of Rei, so the body? Ehh, okay, but what about its scale? Do we include the wings? How much of its physical appearance is a mix between Lilith, Adam, and Rei? Too many variables are at play to say which is which. Probably looked badass though. THIS IS ALL ASSUMING SHE LOOKED ANY DIFFERENT IN THE FIRST PLACE. And as far as what she acted like, there's also no telling. It's very vague on whether or not the Angels are inherently violent or destructive. The best answer is, "No, but some of them are." The reason for that is because of this answer to the Angel's motivations in NGE2: "Some of them were trying to access Lilith and reset all life, some of them had nothing in mind, and some were trying to recover their progenitor Adam." The motive placed directly in the middle of all that is why I say some of them are inherently violent. If you're super bored and don't know what to do, and your first option is to preform attacks of terrorism against a major city, then it's safe to say you're inherently violent, or at least too ignorant on humanity to know what you're doing is... terrorism. Though it's probably safer to say that they're just ignorant. And with all this being said, Lilith (and Adam) were likely the same way.
